## Roster Changes Since Yesterday

Yesterday's report (2026-05-15) did not persist an `active_strategies_snapshot`, so a formal diff is unavailable. No strategy additions, removals, or toggles are detected in `strategies.json` relative to the last known snapshot (2026-05-13, 50 strategies). The roster currently has 48 enabled strategies. All five pending proposals from 2026-05-14 and 2026-05-15 (1 promotion, 4 kill batches) remain PROPOSED and unenacted by the operator.

---

**One strategy clears the full promotion bar for the third consecutive day: v21-bottom-excluded (shadow).** n=208, total net SOL +3.075, drop_top3 +1.074, monthly run rate +18.86 SOL/mo — every gate is green. The nearest panel11 filter analog (vel 20-50 + dd > -10%) shows wr_std_dev=4.5% (STABLE, well below the 15% threshold). ---

**Today's by_strategy_daily_snapshot shows all n_today=0 due to UTC midnight rollover** — no completed trades have settled in the current UTC day at the time of report generation. This is a reporting artifact, not an operational failure: `diagnose.json` confirms HEALTHY (8793 total graduations, last graduation 82s ago, pipeline active). Evaluation uses lifetime leave-one-out data.

---

**Three patterns have recurred across 3+ reporting cycles:**

1. **v21-bottom-excluded promotion delay (Day 3).** This is the only strategy clearing all four SOL-bar gates. The longer it runs in shadow rather than live, the longer the research investment takes to pay back. Every shadow trade is a dry run of a system that appears ready.

It continues to consume entries and pollute the signal pool.

3. **v10-best-double DECAYING watch.** Strong lifetime metrics (n=81, total=+3.930, drop3=+1.606, mo=+8.16) but the recent-30 median has dropped to -17.18% versus the all-time median of -6.15%. This is DECAYING in edge-decay. Leave-one-out drop3 remains positive, so no kill yet — but if drop3 falls to ≤0 at n=100, it would become INVALID. Watch closely.

---

**Outlier robustness check:** v21-bottom-excluded (shadow) has drop_top3=+1.074 SOL — the edge persists after removing its three best trades. This is not a lottery-ticket strategy. In contrast, every enabled v25-bot-excl variant except vel2050 has drop3 deeply negative (calm: -4.138, breakeven: -2.909, tp150: -3.012, mono: -2.359), confirming real structural losses — not outlier-driven noise. The DECAYING flag on this strategy is median-based — recent-30 median of -18.95% vs all-time -8.72%, both negative — which is expected fat-tail behavior for a wide-universe strategy. The critical outlier check (drop_top3=+1.69 SOL) confirms the edge is real and distributed across the trade sample, not concentrated in 1-3 lottery wins. This is not a kill signal; it's a memcoin distribution shape.

**v14-snipers-liq** kill proposal (ai-2026-05-14-002) is also stuck. At n=91, drop_top3=-2.64 SOL, monthly=1.29 SOL/mo, this strategy has no realistic path to the bar. It has appeared on the kill list twice (2026-05-10, 2026-05-14) with no action. Recommend operator execute the kill this cycle.

Worst: v12-vel2050-liq-dd15 (-0.74 SOL, 7 trades), v21-bottom-excluded (-0.44 SOL, 41 trades — expected for a wide-universe strategy at this trade size).

## New Kill Candidates

Four strategies are newly flagged for early kill based on deeply negative trajectories:

1. **v22-snipers-flow** (n=36): drop_top3=-2.47, monthly=-13.8 SOL/mo, DECAYING flag, mean=-9.3%. This was in yesterday's kill narrative but no action item was created. Now escalating to an action item.

- **v10-best-double** (score 95.2): n=76, drop_top3=+1.17, monthly=7.69 SOL/mo. 3/4 gates pass — only n<100 is missing. 24 more trades needed. DECAYING flag on median but fat-tail mean still positive (+9.19%). Closest approach after v21-bottom-excluded.

## Headline

Pipeline stalled (WS zombie, ~2.5h gap) blocked all trades today; two kills executed on clear losers; the calm-survivor cohort is structurally bleeding SOL despite positive medians and is at risk of mass invalidation at n=100.

## What Accumulated SOL / What Bled

No trades today — the graduation listener went zombie sometime before 08:13 UTC (last graduation 8823s before the 11:12 diagnose snapshot). Auto-reconnect was pending at 1308s into the 1500s trigger window. Yesterday saw 368 trades across 171 graduations.

From the canonical leave-one-out-pnl view, no n≥100 strategy clears the promotion bar — all veterans have negative drop_top3 SOL, confirming no graduated edge exists yet. The accumulation story is entirely in the sub-100-n cohort: **v9shadow-vel20-top5** leads at +3.78 SOL (n=39, drop_top3=+0.73, monthly=+10.97 SOL), followed by **v10-best-double** at +3.10 SOL (n=46, drop_top3=+0.80, monthly=+9.89) and **v9shadow-velmono-dev** at +3.10 SOL (n=18). All three pass the outlier-strip test (drop_top3 > 0), which is the critical real-edge signal.

On the negative side, the entire v17/v18 calm-survivor family (BE and Markov overlays) is burning SOL. All variants post negative total_net_sol (−1.22 to −1.41 SOL) despite surface-level positive medians of 7–9%. Root cause: at ~3pp/trade cost, a TP=10 strategy needs >75% WR to break even on SOL. These strategies run 56–62% WR — a structural shortfall that no exit overlay can patch. The last-25-trade median has collapsed to −4% to −9% across all v17/v18 variants, confirming the edge is actively decaying, not just noisy.

## Today's Kills

**v18-calm-mkv-aggressive** (KILLED): HIT-KILL criterion tripped at n=68 (kill_criterion: n≥30 and median<−3; actual median=−8.51%). total_net_sol=−1.22, drop_top3=−1.60, monthly=−16.78 SOL. Grad 20564 hit −91.1% across all calm-survivor strategies simultaneously, confirming Markov matrix abstained (insufficient cell samples) on exactly the worst token type.

**v18-calm-mkv-strict** (KILLED): At n=68, strict and exitonly are statistically identical on every metric (median 8.46%, mean −4.12%, WR 60.3%). Per action item ai-2026-05-10-006 (set at n=50), the hold-side (hold>0.85) is inert. Killing strict; retaining exitonly as the cleaner single-hypothesis version. Both remain SOL-negative.

## Cross-Day Pattern Observations

Two pipeline health incidents in consecutive sessions: T30 callback stall (1111s) on 2026-05-10, WS zombie on 2026-05-11. Both are connection-layer issues. Action item ai-2026-05-10-001 remains unresolved — auto-reconnect masks rather than fixes the root cause. If a third incident occurs next session, the zombie listener should be treated as a systemic reliability bug requiring a deeper fix (keepalive tuning, heartbeat monitoring, or supervisory restart logic).

Grad 20560 produced +39.5% across v9shadow-vel20-top5, v14-sumabs-shadow, and v21-bottom-excluded simultaneously — a broad market pump that lifted multiple unrelated strategies. Grad 20564 produced −91.1% across all v17/v18 calm-survivor variants simultaneously — a single extreme dump that hit every strategy sharing the sum_abs<60 + dd>-10% filter. This symmetry suggests the filter selects for tokens with high bi-directional tail exposure, not for one-sided momentum.

The v17 BE calibration cohort (be5/be7/be9) behaves identically to each other in recent trades. At n=69 all three show median ~+8% lifetime but last-25 median −4% to −5%. The BE trigger level (5/7/9%) is not differentiating outcomes — they're all receiving the same grad flow and sharing losses. Either the BE level doesn't matter much (confirming the be9 hypothesis that BE rarely fires) or all three are hitting the same loss tail on the same tokens.

## Honest Self-Check

The positive medians (+7–9%) on v17/v18 are real in aggregate but misleading for SOL accounting. leave-one-out confirms negative total_net_sol for every variant. This is NOT a fat-tail strategy: drop_top3 is also negative (−1.67 to −1.79 SOL), meaning even after stripping the top 3 winners there is no residual edge — the wins are not fat enough to compensate a fat loss tail. Demanding continued operation to n=100 is justified per protocol (n<100 → WATCH), but the probability of reversal is low given consistent decay.

Top SOL accumulators (v9shadow-vel20-top5, v10-best-double) have positive drop_top3, which IS the right signal. However, they are at n=39 and n=46 — insufficient for promotion confidence. The panel11 regime check on their underlying combos (vel<20+top5<10% wr_std=7.9% STABLE, vel 20-50+dd>-10% wr_std=2.7% STABLE) is encouraging but the panel7 walk-forward flags the individual components as DEGRADED, so combo stability needs to hold through n=100 before trusting the gate.

## Operator Next Step

1. **Confirm WS zombie self-healed** (check Railway dashboard for graduation detections after 11:13 UTC today). If still stalled, restart listener. Data flow is the critical path — without it, v19/v20/v21 cannot reach n=100.
2. v18-calm-mkv-aggressive and v18-calm-mkv-strict kills are pushed in this report's commands and will take effect on the next bot sync (~2 min after this commit).
3. Monitor v17/v18 survivors (be5/be7/be9, mkv-exitonly) at n=80: if still DECAYING and total_net_sol still negative, plan mass INVALID declaration at n=100.

## Headline

A +148.9% outlier trade on graduation 20359 pushed today's shadow P&L to +6.28 SOL, but the bot is running with a T30 pipeline stall (~18 min since last callback) and trade volume halved from yesterday (103 vs 240). Fix the wiring before reading any performance signal.

## What Happened Today

Graduation 20359 (mint: 4k95VBsznoxmq2AmPPmxjf5trBiaLWE2DPYHZqAvpump) pumped +148.9% net and was caught simultaneously by v10-best-double, v20-vel2050-dd-base, and v20-vel2050-dd-sl20 — contributing the bulk of today's +6.28 SOL profit figure. Strip that single event and the remaining ~100 trades reflect normal calm-survivor behavior: median TP exits at ~+10%, SL exits in the −25 to −35% range. All calm-survivor strategies (v17-be5/7/9, v18-mkv variants) showed elevated TP rates today (82–100% vs their 7-day baseline of 40–65%). This is a bullish market-regime signature, not a strategy change — on days like this, the calm-survivor entry filter selects tokens that ride the wave cleanly.

## Pipeline Stall — URGENT

The diagnose verdict is `PIPELINE_STALLED`. The T30 callback was last fired 1111 seconds (~18 minutes) before the snapshot, yet L1–L4 all pass (graduations arriving, prices clean, timestamps OK, labels correct). The PriceCollector → StrategyManager wiring is intermittently silent. This partially explains the volume drop from 240 trades yesterday to 103 today — missed T30 callbacks mean missed entry events. The operator should pull Railway logs for the T30 processing path (look for a stalled Promise, uncaught rejection, or a dead event listener), restart if needed, and confirm the callback resumes firing before trusting today's per-strategy counts.

## Leaderboard Evolution — Important Shift

The most significant analytical update: `vel < 5 + dev < 3%` — listed in CLAUDE.md as the first confirmed n≥100 winner at +3.09% — has now grown to n=161 and its opt_avg_ret has fallen to −1.63%. It still beats the −10.96% baseline, but the edge has compressed dramatically as sample size increased from 125 to 161. This is a textbook small-n overfitting story. The new top-two n≥100 combos with regime-stable verification are:

1. **`vel 20-50 + dd > -10%`** — n=114, opt=+6.89% @ tp=150/sl=30, **STABLE** wr_std_dev=2.9% (best regime stability in the catalog)
2. **`age > 10min + top5 < 10%`** — n=119, opt=+6.38% @ tp=150/sl=30, **MODERATE** wr_std_dev=8.1%

Both are already deployed: v20-vel2050-dd-base mirrors #1 with corrected gate (+5..+100) and SL (30), and v19-age10-top5-base mirrors #2. CLAUDE.md baseline section should be updated to reflect this shift and retire the `vel<5+dev<3%` promotion claim.

## v17/v18 Calm-Survivor Cohort at n=33–34

All seven BE/Markov variants are ON-TRACK. Medians cluster between +7.38% (mkv-aggressive) and +9.53% (be9). The key finding this session: **v18-mkv-strict and v18-mkv-exitonly are statistically identical** at n=33 (median=9.04%, mean=2.06%, WR=72.7%, exit-mix: tp=24/sl=1 each). The Markov hold-side (hold>0.85 vs unreachable >0.99) is contributing zero value. At n=50, the plan is to keep mkv-exitonly and kill mkv-strict unless divergence appears. On the BE side, v17-calm-be9 is leading at median=9.53%, WR=76.5% — the "upper-bound control" expected to be inert has unexpectedly emerged as the best performer. This suggests the BE@+9% is acting as a late-stage position lock rather than a rescue mechanism, which merits tracking to n=100 before a formal promotion decision.

## Median Cross-Check (Honest)

Several mean figures today are outlier-driven and should not be read as edge signals:
- **v20-vel2050-dd-base mean=114.08%** from exactly 2 trades — both hit the same big pump. n=2 is noise.
- **v9shadow-velmono-dev median=51.10%, v9shadow-velmid-liq-mono median=49.00%** — both LOW-N (<25). Extraordinary numbers but unfalsifiable at current n.

- The exit_mix anomalies (TP rate 82–100% across v17/v18 today vs 40–65% baseline) are expected on a bullish market day, not a structural signal.

The roster has accumulated clearly failed experiments that add noise to daily stats. The following block should be deleted:
- **v11-vel5dev3-liq100** (n=9, median=−20.75%): liq overlay traps on peak-momentum tokens that immediately reverse
- **v11-age10dev3-liq100** (n=9, median=−20.75%): same failure mode, different entry filter
- **v11-vel5dev3-t30/t120/t240** (late-entry research): confirmed negative or near-zero median across all three horizons (−1.10%, −4.89%, −4.32%). The late-entry hypothesis failed universally.
- **v14-snipers-liq** (n=4, 0% WR, median=−19.26%): directional signal is unambiguous even at low-n
- **v12-bcage1d-dd10** (n=21, median=−4.23%, WR=33%): sparkline declining, macro regime doesn't reward old-token tokens

recent_reports.narrative:
## First daily-report on record — Markov exits fail out of the gate, v16-calm-base takes a regime hit, but the weekly trend is the best seen to date.

**Today in numbers:** 119 shadow trades across 60 graduations, net −4.31 SOL. That follows 271 trades yesterday — the reduced count reflects a quieter graduation day, not a pipeline fault (diagnose: HEALTHY). The daily P&L is negative but manageable; the story is in the per-strategy breakdown.

**What worked:** Two graduations — 20054 and 20056 — carried the entire winner side. Graduation 20054 pumped cleanly over the 5-minute hold, netting +37.9% on both v14-sumabs-shadow and v14-sumabs-dd-shadow via timeout. Graduation 20056 hit the vol-30-60 filter's TP in 22 seconds flat — +33.5% net on v9shadow-vol-30-60 and v13-vol3060-dd10. These are the clean pump-and-hold outcomes the calm-entry filters are designed to catch. The v14-sumabs-dd-shadow now sits at all-time median +9.18% over n=10 — the cleanest live signal among mature overlays with a dd protection layer.

**What failed:** Two catastrophic tokens dominated the loser side. Graduation 20145 crushed every v17 and v18 position simultaneously — all exited at −45.1% within 53 seconds via SL or markov_exit. Graduation 20146 destroyed v11 late-entry positions at −55% to −57% in 49–139 seconds. The Markov exit mechanic in v18 (strict and exitonly) fired exits on graduation 20145 that aligned exactly with the SL path — providing zero rescue benefit over a plain stop-loss. At n=9, it is too early to formally kill v18, but the thesis (Markov should trim the loss tail) is not validating. Critically: the v18-exitonly variant (hold threshold 0.99, effectively unreachable) performed identically to v18-strict (both median −23.56%). Since exitonly isolates the early-exit mechanic while disabling the hold-side, this confirms the early-exit itself is causing harm — same failure mode as v16-be3, which caught noise pullbacks inside the natural volatility band. v17 BE variants (be5/be7/be9) all show median −18.56% at n=10 today, all driven by the same graduation 20145 event. The control case (be9, near-inert BE one tick before TP) matching be5 and be7 exactly confirms the damage was token-level, not BE-mechanism-specific.

**v16-calm-base regime hit:** The core v16 strategy dropped from yesterday's daily median +8.03% to today's −6.61% (delta −14.64pp). Exit mix shifted: TP rate fell from 61% to 42%, SL rate rose from 27% to 42%. This looks like a single-day market regime event — the 7-day edge-decay sparkline shows STABLE, and the all-time median is +7.76% (n=56, journal: ON-TRACK). v16-calm-trail5 tells a different story: all-time median +0.61% vs base's +7.76% at the same n=56. The trailing stop (activate +5%, distance 3%) is confirmed dead weight — it is not catching runners and is costing win rate. At n=100, if median stays below +4%, kill trail5 and do not reiterate trailing stops below act+8%/dist+5%.

**Leaderboard shift — CLAUDE.md needs updating:** Two combos have crossed n=100 since the last snapshot:
1. `vel 20-50 + dd > −10%` — n=111, opt +5.13% @ tp=150/sl=30, **STABLE** regime (wr_std_dev=2.8%). The prior snapshot noted n=92, opt +7.58% @ 150/25 — opt has settled lower as more diverse-regime data accumulated, and optimal SL shifted from 25 to 30. Still beats baseline (−11.2%) by over 16pp and is the most regime-stable combo in the catalog. The live v10-best-double (median −4.34% at n=37) severely underperforms research (+5.13%) — likely due to entry gate mismatch and possible TP/SL misconfiguration.
2. `age > 10min + top5 < 10%` — n=117, opt +6.17% @ tp=150/sl=30, **MODERATE** regime (wr_std_dev=10.6%). New leaderboard leader. No live shadow strategy exists. Deploying a shadow strategy here is the single highest-value action available.
Note: `vel < 5 + dev < 3%` has decayed from opt +3.09% (n=125, 2026-05-01) to −1.44% (n=155, today). Still beats baseline, but the prior 'first true winner' characterization overstated the edge — more regime-diverse data eroded the opt.

**Weekly trend — genuinely positive:** W16: median −31.5% → W17: −9.9% → W18: −15.1% → W19 (partial): −4.26%. W19 is the best weekly median since trading began. The calm-survivor filter family is producing a materially better return distribution.

**Mean vs median audit:** Several strategies show dangerous divergences. v9shadow-vel20-top5: mean +24.93% but median +2.43% (n=27) — sparkline shows 140% and 95% outlier wins early in history. v12-bcage1d-dd10: mean +26.31%, median −3.5% — early 179%/193% outlier trades. v11-vel5dev3-liq100: mean +12.67%, median −20.75% at n=9. None of these means are trustworthy. The median is the honest read in all three cases.

**Operator next step:** Push a `strategy-commands.json` to `main` with: (1) upsert `v19-age10-top5-shadow` for `age>10min + top5<10%` at tp=150/sl=30, entry gate +5..+100, 0.5 SOL shadow; (2) verify and correct v10-best-double's TP/SL to tp=150/sl=30 matching the current leaderboard optimum. These two actions address the highest-value gap in the current portfolio.
